schema.description: List of suspension records for a company.

items.description: Record representing the suspension of a company's Gusto account.

company_uuid.description: Unique identifier for the company which is suspended.

effective_date.description: Date that the suspension took effect.

leaving_for.description: Which competitor the company is joining instead. Only required if `reason` is `'switching_provider'`.

reason.description: Explanation for why the company's account was suspended.

reconcile_tax_method.description: How Gusto will handle taxes already collected.

file_quarterly_forms.description: Should Gusto file quarterly tax forms on behalf of the company? The correct answer can depend on why the company is suspending their account, and how taxes are being reconciled.

file_yearly_forms.description: Should Gusto file yearly tax forms on behalf of the company? The correct answer can depend on why the company is suspending their account, and how taxes are being reconciled.

comments.description: User-supplied comments describing why they are suspending their account.

tax_refunds.description: Describes the taxes which are refundable to the company for this suspension. These may be refunded or paid by Gusto depending on the value in `reconcile_tax_method`.

description.description: What kind of tax this is.

items.title: Company-Suspension

success_status.comments: Company is transitioning to ADP for their payroll and HR needs

schema.title: Company-Suspension-List

404.description:
Not Found

The requested resource does not exist. Make sure the provided UUID is valid.

schema.description:
Not Found 
  
The requested resource does not exist. Make sure the provided ID/UUID is valid.

error_key.description: Specifies where the error occurs. Typically this key identifies the attribute/parameter related to the error.

category.description: Specifies the type of error. The category provides error groupings and can be used to build custom error handling in your integration.

message.description: Provides details about the error - generally this message can be surfaced to an end user.

errors.message: The requested resource was not found.

schema.title: Not-Found-Error-Object
